name: Gravel from the balcony of Penedès
name_original: Gravel desde el balcón del Penedès
description:
Starting from Vilafranca del Penedès, this route proposes to cross the central part of the Penedès plain towards the northwest, gradually gaining altitude until reaching the Miravinya del Balcó del Penedès viewpoint. Located in the Serra del Bolet, the ascent to this viewpoint presents a long and practically sustained ascent that rewards with excellent views over the Penedès plain, but also the Garraf, and on days of good visibility, even the Barcelones.

The route begins with an almost unexpected path, just after leaving the Vilafranca sports area, and which follows the Llitrà stream. You will enter Pacs del Penedès by another path, which follows the Pacs stream and leads to the racó de la Salzareda, a place with good shade next to the church of Sant Genís. Leaving Pacs behind, another flat covered with vineyards opens up and, in the distance, you can see the small mountain of Can Sogues, on whose slopes lies the town of Vilobí del Penedès and where the Parc dels Talls is located.

This park is the result of the recovery of gypsum mines and has several lakes that flood the old quarries. On the other side of the Can Sogues mountain there is another new flat covered with vineyards and, further on, the Serra del Bolet, where the highest point of the route awaits you.

The route now surrounds the nucleus of the Rovira Roja to then begin a 7-kilometre ascent in which you will only find a short break. It is not a very hard ascent, although there are specific slopes where it will be convenient to engage the large crown and in other places choose a good line to avoid any large stones that appear on the way. The Font-rubí fountain is a good place to take a short break, refill the drums and thus face an ascent section of almost 2 kilometers on asphalt to reach the Miravinya Balcó del Penedès.

After reaching the viewpoint, the path continues to climb a little more, bordering the Sierra del Bolet and leaving the entire Penedès plain on your right. The descent to l'Avellà requires a little attention. The track is in good condition, but the descent is steep, with a few hairpin bends where you will find some loose stone, so it is advisable not to go down too fast. After crossing the small town of l'Avellà and the road, you will continue descending again towards the flat, the track in its first section is steep and you will find stones, so, again, I recommend caution.

The next section, until reaching Santa María de Bellver, is a succession of farmhouses, some isolated, others very close to each other, forming small nuclei or "neighborhoods" on an undulating plateau that outlines the transition between the Serra del Bolet and the flat of the Penedes. Again you will find some section of steep descent, so be careful! Crossing streams, between vineyards, farmhouses and cellars, you reach Puigdàlber, which you will cross quickly until you reach Santa Fe del Penedès. Here the path deviates just before entering the town to go through the Torrent de la Casa Nova path, a good time to take advantage of the shade and the radical change in the landscape. Before leaving Santa Fe, you will go to the Parc dels Pins de Cal Ferret, a recreational area whose fountain and viewpoint are worth a visit.

After a brief ascent by road leaving Santa Fe, the route continues between vineyards until you can see Vilafranca del Penedès again, where the route ends.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Vilafranca Del Penedès offers diverse terrain, characterized by extensive vineyards and rural paths. The landscape transitions towards the foothills of the Garraf massif to the south, providing varied elevations and views. The region also features the Foix marsh and the Ordal Mountains, part of the Catalan coastal range, contributing to a rich tapestry of environments. This area connects vineyard plains with coastal and forested sections, creating varied routes for gravel cyclists. Best gravel bike trails around Vilafranca Del Penedès The most popular gravel bike trail is The south of the plain in gravel, a difficult 42.4 miles (68.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route explores the agricultural plains and offers extensive vineyard scenery. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Gravel through Penedès, the capital of cava, a difficult 32.8 miles (52.7 km) path. This route winds through the heart of the Penedès wine region, passing numerous vineyards and wineries. Local gravel bikers also love the Gravel from the balcony of Penedès, a 36.1 miles (58.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with elevated viewpoints, often completed in about 5 hours 10 minutes. Gravel biking around Vilafranca Del Penedès is defined by vineyard-covered plains, rural dirt tracks, and sections with views towards the Garraf massif and the coast. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more accessible paths. The routes in Vilafranca Del Penedès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Vilafranca Del Penedès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Vilafranca Del Penedès?
A: There are over 50 gravel bike trails around Vilafranca Del Penedès. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, offering diverse experiences across the region's landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Vilafranca Del Penedès?
A: The terrain primarily features extensive vineyard-covered plains and rural dirt tracks. As you head south, the landscape shifts towards the foothills of the Garraf massif and the Ordal Mountains, introducing more varied elevations and some challenging climbs. You can also find sections with views towards the coast.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly gravel bike routes in Vilafranca Del Penedès?
A: While many routes are rated difficult, there are 11 moderate routes available that are suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. These often traverse the gentler vineyard plains, providing a good introduction to gravel biking in the region.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see along the gravel trails?
A: Many trails offer panoramic views of the vineyards and towards the Garraf massif. You can also find routes that pass by significant landmarks. For instance, the Foix marsh and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt; are notable sights. Some routes also lead to elevated points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2579854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any gravel routes that offer sea views?
A: Yes, some routes extend towards the coast, providing glimpses of the Mediterranean. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e840487889/gravel-with-sea-views-in-penedes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel with sea views in Penedes&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to incorporate these coastal perspectives, blending vineyard scenery with distant ocean views.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in the area?
A: Many of the gravel bike routes in Vilafranca Del Penedès are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. This makes planning your ride and logistics, such as parking, much simpler.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Vilafranca Del Penedès?
A: The Penedès region is enjoyable for gravel biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with the vineyards in full bloom or displaying rich autumnal colors. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to ride, though some tracks might be damp after rain. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended.

Q: Are there opportunities to combine gravel biking with wine tasting or visiting wineries?
A: Absolutely. Vilafranca Del Penedès is the 'Capital of Wine,' and many gravel routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e842296267/gravel-through-penedes-the-capital-of-cava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel through Penedès, the capital of cava&lt;/a&gt;, wind directly through vineyards and past historic bodegas. This offers excellent opportunities to stop for wine tastings or explore the region's rich wine culture as part of your ride.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Vilafranca Del Penedès?
A: The gravel biking trails in Vilafranca Del Penedès are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the extensive vineyard scenery, the variety of terrain, and the well-maintained rural tracks that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging gravel routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e840866966/gravel-to-the-highest-of-penedes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel to the highest of Penedes&lt;/a&gt; covers over 80 km with more than 1200 meters of ascent, taking you to some of the highest points in the region.

Q: Is it possible to access the gravel trails using public transport?
A: Vilafranca Del Penedès is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train from Barcelona, making it feasible to reach the starting points of many routes without a car. The town itself serves as a central hub, and many trails begin directly from or near the urban area.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a gravel bike tour?
A: Vilafranca Del Penedès offers various parking options within the town, including public car parks. Many routes are designed to be accessible from the town center, allowing you to park centrally and easily begin your ride into the surrounding vineyards and natural areas.
tips: We are a few meters from the urban center of Vilafranca del Penedès, but entering this narrow path, surrounded by vegetation, which runs parallel to the Riera de Llitrà, it seems that we are very far from civilization. Suitable for mountain bikes, gravel or hiking.
Trail that runs along the Pacs stream and connects with the Pont de la Salzereda and the church of Sant Genís. It is a quiet stretch and surrounded by lush vegetation.

From this viewpoint we can see the largest lake in the Parc dels Talls. These lakes, known as "pèlags", have been formed by the phreatic flooding of old gypsum quarries, in an example of the renaturalization and recovery of a mining area. The area is very rich in diversity of flora and fauna.

The Font-rubí fountain is located in a truly strategic place: it is the highest point of Alt de Font-rubí for road cyclists, but also for mountain and gravel cyclists who go up the track that comes from La Rovira Red. And from here you can continue going up, in the direction of Coll de la Barraca or Serra del Bolet, and also reach the Miravinya Balcó del Penedès viewpoint.
Part of the 5 Miravinya spread over the Alt Penedès, the Miravinya Balcó del Penedès offers a panoramic view of the Penedès Plain from the Serra del Bolet.

This wooded path runs alongside the Casa Nova torrent, has a small rest area with a bench and is a quiet and cool place. It is not technically difficult, as it is wide and the track has no stones, but there is a fairly steep short slope at the end if we go in the direction of l'Alzinaret.

Urban park located in Santa Fe del Penedès, formerly this area was part of the Cal Ferret mansion, but it was ceded to the municipality by its owners and is currently a recreation area, with benches, barbecue, service building, and a fountain. In the viewpoint there is an information panel.
The narrow path that comes from Les Cabanyes lets us guess Vilafranca del Penedès in this spectacular view.

waypoints.name: View of the Largest Lake in Parc dels Talls
Trail of the Torrent de la Casa Nova
